preFix.title: Adding a calico undercover to a chair seat
preFix.subtitle: Staple an unbleached calico layer over the filled seat pad before the top cover goes on
preFix.excerpt: How to add a calico undercover to a drop-in or fixed chair seat. The calico layer holds the filling in shape, prevents the top cover fabric from wearing against the filling, and gives a smooth base for the final cover.

content.text: is a standard intermediate layer in traditional upholstery. Fitting it well takes the same technique as fitting the top cover, the difference is that calico is cheap enough to practise on, and any mistakes are hidden. Getting the undercover smooth and taut is good practice for the top cover that follows.
Step 1, Cut the calico to size

Measure the seat pad at its widest and longest points and add 80 mm all round, this gives enough to wrap under the seat frame and staple. Cut one piece with the upholstery shears. Cutting calico on the grain (following the weave threads) avoids the diagonal distortion that makes it pull uneven.
Step 2, Pull and staple using the correct sequence
Lay the calico over the seat pad and hold the seat frame in your lap. Follow the

: pull the centre of the front edge taut and set one staple. Move to the centre of the back, pull firm and straight, set one staple. Pull the centre of each side, set one staple each. Now work outward from each centre staple toward the corners, pulling the calico smooth as you go. Use 8 mm T50 staples at 40 to 50 mm spacing.
Step 3, Ease and cut the corners

At each corner, the excess calico bunches into a fold. Cut a notch from the corner of the fabric (not through to the stapled edge, leave 20 mm) so the two flaps can lie flat. Fold one flap over the other to make a mitre and staple both down. The corner should lie flat without a lump. If the fold raises above the seat surface, the notch was not deep enough, ease out the corner staples with the staple remover and recut.
Run your hand across the surface of the covered seat. Any lumps or hollow spots can be adjusted through the calico with the

still in place, push the regulator into the calico and move the filling beneath it. This is much easier than opening the calico once it is stapled. A smooth, slightly domed surface (not flat, the dome is what gives a seat its fullness) is the target before the top cover goes on.
